passage.title: Robot Path Sign Project
passage.text: The robot path team wanted children to understand start and stop steps more clearly. At first, the group posted a tool chart that packed several actions into one square, but children could not tell the order of the tech steps at one glance. Tech helpers said "There are too many actions in one box, and I miss the order." After hearing that, the team used one strong icon for each app and kept the screen style the same from beginning to end. They also placed one extra step sign before the final tap so the last reminder repeated the final action before mistakes happened. On the next test day, children moved through the robot steps more steadily after the redesign. The team noticed that younger students needed strong icons and shorter directions. Because of that result, the school chose to apply the new sign style to more digital activities.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Writing long reports about class rules
Making digital steps easier for children to understand
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Children could not tell the order of the tech steps
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group set near the charging shelf?
choices: Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
A quick-help board
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does digital mean in the last sentence?
choices: A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Using electronic tools
A room that is easy to clean
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It removed every sign from the area
Shorter clues and stronger icons made the steps easier to follow
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Extend the new sign style to more digital activities
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
